paper_title: The effect of high dust amount on the surface temperature during the Last Glacial Maximum: A modelling study using MIROC-ESM

methods.description: Design and execution of climate experiments using the MIROC-ESM model to simulate different climate conditions, including pre-industrial and Last Glacial Maximum scenarios.

steps.description: We performed eight experiments: five using the AGCM part of the MIROC-ESM and three using the full MIROC-ESM.
The specific experiments labelled PI.a and PI.e represent the AD 1850 control climate of the PI era, with PI.e having been submitted to CMIP5.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate AD 1850 control climate for PI era

steps.description: The previous 100-year climatology of sea surface temperature (SST) and sea ice of the period submitted to CMIP5 was used as a boundary condition for PI.a.
steps.searchable_summary: use 100-year climatology of SST and sea ice as boundary condition

steps.description: The experiments labelled LGM.e and LGM.a represent the LGM climate following the PMIP3 protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate LGM climate following PMIP3 protocol

steps.description: The LGMglac.a experiment was a new experiment based on the same conditions as LGM.a, but with an additional glaciogenic dust flux following Mahowald et al. (2006a).
steps.searchable_summary: simulate LGM climate with glaciogenic dust flux

steps.description: The LGMglac.e experiment was the full ESM version of LGMglac.a, which branched from the LGM.e experiment 40 years prior to the period submitted to CMIP5-PMIP3.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct full ESM version of LGMglac.a

methods.description: Analysis of dust emission fluxes and comparison with data archives to understand dust sources and their impact during different climate scenarios.

steps.description: The emission flux of dust (g m −2 yr −1 ) is shown in Fig. 2 for the PI.a, LGM.a, and LGMglac.a experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze dust emission flux for different experiments

steps.description: The global dust budget can be compared with the findings of previous studies.
steps.searchable_summary: compare global dust budget with previous studies

steps.description: The change in the zonal mean dust loading in the atmosphere for the ratios LGM.a / PI.a and LGMglac.a / PI a is shown in Fig. 3a and b, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze change in zonal mean dust loading
